originalTitle: Cruise passengers tell of life on board ship stranded off Cape Verde after hantavirus outbreak
title: A Big Ship Stuck at Sea!
content:
Hello, My Young Explorers!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you exciting (but sometimes tricky!) stories from across our amazing continent of Africa and its neighbours. Today, we're talking about a very special ship that is on a big adventure, but it's a bit stuck right now. Imagine a giant floating hotel, full of people, out in the wide, blue ocean! That's what we're talking about. We are going to learn all about it.

A Big Ship on a Big Ocean

There is a big ship called the MV Hondius. It's not just any ship; it's a special kind of ship called a cruise ship. On a cruise ship, people go on holidays. They eat yummy food, play games, and see beautiful places. The MV Hondius was sailing near a lovely group of islands called Cape Verde. Cape Verde is not far from Africa, right in the big Atlantic Ocean. People on the ship were having a good time. They were seeing the ocean and maybe even some cool sea animals. Did you know? Cape Verde is a country made of ten islands! It's very sunny there, and people love to sing and dance.

Why is the Ship Stuck?

So, why is this big ship not moving? Well, the ship is "stranded." This means it cannot go to a port. A port is like a big parking lot for ships, where people get on and off. The MV Hondius is staying out at sea for a little longer. This is because some people on the ship got a bit sick. Grown-ups want to be super careful. They want to make sure no one else gets sick before the ship comes to land. They are working hard to keep everyone safe and healthy.

What is "Hantavirus"? A Tiny Germ Story

Some people on the ship got sick with something called "Hantavirus." That's a big word! Let's make it simple. Hantavirus is like a tiny, tiny germ. You cannot see it with your eyes. These germs sometimes live with very small animals, like certain kinds of mice or rats. If you touch where these animals have been, or breathe in tiny bits of dust where they live, you can catch the germ. When people get Hantavirus, it can make them feel like they have a very bad cold or flu. They might feel tired, have a headache, or a tummy ache. Did you know? Hantavirus is quite rare. It means not many people get it. But it's very important to wash your hands often and stay away from places where mice or rats might live. Always tell a grown-up if you see them!

Life on the Ship Right Now

So, what are the people on the MV Hondius doing while they are stuck at sea? The passengers say they are calm. This is good news! It means they are not scared. They are waiting patiently. Imagine being on a big floating hotel for extra days! They are still getting food and drinks. The grown-ups on the ship, like the captains and the doctors, are making sure everyone is comfortable. They are playing games, reading books, and maybe watching movies. They might be looking out at the big ocean, too. It's like an extra-long holiday, but maybe not the kind they planned!

Who is Helping Everyone?

Many smart and kind grown-ups are helping with the MV Hondius. These people are called "officials." Officials are like super helpers! They work for the government and health groups. Their job is to keep everyone safe. Doctors on the ship are checking on all the passengers and the crew. A crew is like the team that works on the ship. These doctors are making sure that if anyone feels unwell, they get help quickly. They are looking to see if the tiny germ, the Hantavirus, has spread to other people. They want to be super sure everyone is healthy before the ship comes to a port.

What Happens Next?

The MV Hondius will stay at sea for a few more days. This gives the doctors enough time to check everyone. When they are sure that no one else is sick, and that it is safe, the ship will sail to a special port. At this port, all the people will be able to get off the ship. They will then travel home to their families. It's a bit like waiting for a green light to cross the road. You have to wait until it is safe. Our friends on the MV Hondius are waiting for their safe green light!

Keeping Safe and Healthy!

This story reminds us that sometimes, tiny germs can cause big changes to our plans. But it also shows us how grown-ups work together to solve problems and keep everyone safe. It's super important for all of us, no matter where we are, to always wash our hands with soap and water. Especially before eating and after playing! And if you ever feel unwell, always tell a grown-up. They will know what to do to help you feel better. We send our best wishes to all the people on the MV Hondius. We hope they get home soon! Keep exploring, stay curious, and always remember to be kind!
summary: A big ship called MV Hondius is stuck near Cape Verde because some people got sick with a tiny germ called Hantavirus, and doctors are checking everyone to make sure they are safe before letting them go home.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows how grown-ups work hard to keep everyone safe when germs make people sick, and it reminds us to always wash our hands to stay healthy.

originalTitle: Orphaned baby hippo to be hand-reared by keepers at Kenya sanctuary
title: Meet Bumpy: A Special Baby Hippo Story in Kenya!
content:
Hello, my awesome friends! Get ready for a super special story all the way from Africa, from a beautiful country called Kenya. It's about a little baby animal who needed lots of help, and kind people are making sure he gets it!

Who is Bumpy the Baby Hippo?

Imagine a tiny, pinkish-grey baby. He has small ears, big wet eyes, and a very cute face. This is Bumpy! He is a baby hippo, and he's just too cute for words. But Bumpy had a very sad start to his life, and that's why he needs our help to tell his story. Bumpy was found by some grown-ups near a big lake in Kenya. Lakes are like giant puddles where lots of water animals live. But Bumpy was not playing. He was clinging to his mommy, who was very, very still. Sadly, Bumpy's mommy had passed away, and he was all alone. When a baby animal loses its mother, we say it's an "orphan." It means they don't have their mommy or daddy to look after them. And just like human babies, baby animals need their mommies to teach them everything and keep them safe.

A New Safe Home at the Sanctuary

When the kind people found Bumpy, they knew he couldn't stay alone. He was too little and helpless. So, they took him to a very special place called a wildlife sanctuary. You can think of a sanctuary as a super-duper safe home for animals who are hurt, lost, or orphaned. This special sanctuary is in Kenya, a country in Africa. Africa is a huge continent with many amazing animals, like lions, elephants, giraffes, and, of course, hippos! The sanctuary's job is to look after animals and help them get strong again. Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has deserts, rainforests, and huge grasslands where lots of animals live.

Meet the Kind Keepers: Bumpy's New Family!

At the sanctuary, Bumpy met his new family: the animal keepers! Keepers are like very special animal doctors and parents all rolled into one. They love animals very much and know how to take care of them. For Bumpy, the keepers will be like his new mommy and daddy. They will make sure he eats well, sleeps safely, and learns all the things a little hippo needs to know. This is called "hand-rearing" because the keepers will feed him and look after him by hand, just like a human baby. Hand-rearing takes a lot of time and patience. The keepers will wake up in the middle of the night to feed Bumpy, just like your parents might have done when you were a baby! They will give him special milk, just right for a baby hippo, from a big bottle.

What Do Baby Hippos Need?

Baby hippos, like Bumpy, need lots of things to grow big and strong. First, they need their mother's milk. The keepers will give Bumpy special milk that is full of good things to help him grow. They also need warmth and comfort. Imagine being a tiny baby all alone. It would be scary! So, the keepers will make sure Bumpy feels safe and loved. They will talk to him gently and spend lots of time with him so he doesn't feel lonely. Did you know? A baby hippo is called a calf, and when they are born, they can weigh as much as 50 kilograms! That's like carrying two big bags of potatoes!

Fun Hippo Facts!

Let's learn some cool things about hippos, like Bumpy, to understand them better! Hippos are very interesting animals. Even though they look big and round, they are amazing swimmers! They spend most of their day in the water to keep their skin from getting too hot in the sun. Their eyes, ears, and nostrils (that's their nose!) are on top of their heads, so they can see, hear, and breathe even when most of their body is under the water. It's like having a built-in snorkel! Did you know? Hippos can hold their breath underwater for up to 5 minutes! That's a long time! At night, hippos come out of the water to eat grass. Yes, they eat grass, just like cows! Even though they look very big and strong, they are plant-eaters, which we call herbivores. Hippos have very thick skin, which helps protect them. Sometimes, their skin makes a special pinkish-red liquid that looks like sweat but actually protects their skin from the sun and keeps it moist. It's like natural sunscreen!

Bumpy's Bright Future

Bumpy is very lucky to be at the sanctuary with such kind keepers. They will look after him every single day, making sure he gets bigger and stronger. It will take a long time, maybe many years, for Bumpy to grow up. As he grows, the keepers will teach him how to be a hippo. They will encourage him to swim, to eat grass, and to learn about other animals. Maybe one day, when he is big and strong, Bumpy will even be able to live with other hippos in a special, safe area. This story of Bumpy reminds us that every life is important, and it's wonderful when people help animals who are in trouble. Thanks to the keepers and the sanctuary in Kenya, Bumpy has a second chance at life, full of hope and happiness. Let's all cheer for Bumpy and wish him the best as he grows into a big, strong, and happy hippo!
summary: A baby hippo named Bumpy, found orphaned after his mother passed away in Kenya, is now being hand-reared by kind keepers at a wildlife sanctuary, who will feed and care for him until he can grow big and strong.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows how important it is for people to help baby animals who have lost their parents and need a safe home, giving them a chance to live and grow in Africa.

originalTitle: Police officer tells of operation to retrieve human remains from crocodile
title: Our Brave Police Officer and the Crocodile Clue!
content:
Hello everyone! It's your friendly reporter, Zola, here with exciting news from our wonderful continent of Africa. Today, we're going to talk about brave police officers, big crocodiles, and how science helps solve important mysteries.

Meet Our Police Heroes!

Our police officers are very special people. They work hard every day to keep us safe. They help us when we are lost, they make sure rules are followed, and sometimes, they even help solve big puzzles, just like detectives in a storybook! They are like real-life superheroes wearing uniforms. They are always ready to help families and communities. It's a very important job, full of courage and kindness. Did you know? Police officers also teach kids about safety, like how to cross the road or what to do if you get lost.

About Our Big Friends, the Crocodiles

In many parts of Africa, we have big rivers and lakes. And guess who lives there? Our amazing, ancient friends – crocodiles! Crocodiles are very old animals, they have been on Earth for a very, very long time. They are excellent swimmers and can stay underwater for a long time. Crocodiles are wild animals. This means they live in nature, not in our homes. They are very strong and have powerful jaws. They love to eat fish and other animals they can find in the water. We must always remember to respect wild animals and keep a safe distance from them. Did you know? A crocodile's skin is very tough, like armour! It helps protect them. Did you know? Crocodiles can grow to be very, very big, sometimes longer than a car!

A Very Special Search

Sometimes, sad things happen. A person might go missing, and their family worries a lot. Our police officers work very hard to find clues and help bring answers to these families. Recently, in a place near a big river, police officers started a very important search. They were looking for clues about a man who was missing. This was a difficult job because it involved a wild animal – a big crocodile! Police officers had to be very careful and very brave to do this special search. They worked with other experts who know a lot about crocodiles. Together, they made a plan to safely find important information. It was like a real-life treasure hunt, but instead of gold, they were looking for clues to help a family.

Carefully Catching a Crocodile

When something very important happens and involves a wild animal like a crocodile, special animal experts and police officers sometimes need to catch the animal. They do this very carefully, making sure the crocodile is safe and the people are safe too. This is not something anyone should ever try to do on their own. It takes a lot of training and special tools. They only do this when it's super important to find answers or help someone. It's all about being responsible and smart when dealing with nature.

What is DNA? (A Super Secret ID!)

Our police officers found some important things inside the crocodile. To figure out who these things belonged to, they used something called DNA. DNA is like a super tiny, super secret identification card that every living thing has! Every person, every animal, and every plant has its own special DNA. It's like a unique blueprint for them. It tells us who they are. No two people (except identical twins!) have the exact same DNA. So, scientists can look at DNA and know for sure if something belongs to a certain person. Did you know? DNA is so tiny, you need a special microscope to see it! It's like a secret code inside our bodies.

Solving the Mystery with Science!

So, the police officers sent the things they found to a special science lab. In the lab, smart scientists looked at the DNA. They compared it to DNA from the missing man's family. This helps them find out if what they found belongs to the missing man. This is a very important step to bring answers to the worried family. It shows us how science, like DNA testing, can help our police officers solve big mysteries and help people who are sad and searching for answers.

Staying Safe Near Water

Because we have amazing wild animals like crocodiles in Africa, it's very important to be careful. Always, always, always stay away from the edges of rivers, lakes, and other water bodies where crocodiles might live. Never swim in places where crocodiles are known to be. Always go with an adult when you are near water. Listen to grown-ups and follow their rules. They know how to keep you safe from dangers you might not even see. Being smart about safety helps everyone. Did you know? Crocodiles like to hide just under the water's surface, so you might not even see them coming!

Respecting Our Wild Neighbors

Crocodiles are a big part of Africa's nature. They are important for our environment. We must learn to live alongside them safely. This means admiring them from far away, never trying to feed them, and never going into their homes (the water). It’s all about respecting our wild neighbors and understanding that they have their place in the world. And it's about our amazing police and scientists who work together to keep us safe and solve tough puzzles. So, remember, be brave like our police, be curious like our scientists, and always be safe near water! Zola out!
summary: A brave police officer in Africa is using science, specifically DNA tests, to help solve the mystery of a missing man after important clues were found relating to a crocodile.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows how brave police officers and clever science work together to help families find answers when someone goes missing, and it reminds us to be safe and respect wild animals like crocodiles.

originalTitle: Indiana, Ohio primaries draw midterm battle lines, reinforce Trump’s pull
title: Choosing New Helpers in America!
content:
Hello, wonderful friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter, bringing you exciting news from our big, beautiful world! Today, we're going to talk about something happening very, very far away, in a country called America. Imagine if your school had to choose new helpers, like a new class monitor or a new captain for your football team. Well, grown-ups in America do something similar, but for their whole country! It's like a big game where they pick the best players.

What are 'Try-Outs'?

Guess what? Before the *main* big game, teams often have 'try-outs', right? This is where they see who is the strongest and best player to be on their team. In America, they have something like these 'try-outs' for grown-ups who want to be special helpers for their country. They are called 'primary elections'. It's not the final choice yet, but it's a very important step! People who belong to a certain 'team' – like Team Red or Team Blue – choose *who* from their team will get to go to the bigger, final competition. So, these 'try-outs' help them pick the best person to represent their team later on. Did you know? America is a very big country with 50 different parts called 'states'! Each state gets to choose its own special helpers.

Where Did This Happen?

Recently, these special 'try-outs' happened in two places in America. One place is called *Indiana*, and the other is called *Ohio*. These are two of America's many states, just like we have different towns and regions in our own countries. In Indiana and Ohio, grown-ups went to special places to vote. They put papers into boxes to choose who they wanted to represent their teams. Imagine if you lived in a big school with many classes. Your class might choose one person, and another class chooses another person. That's a bit like what happened in Indiana and Ohio. The people in those places chose their favourite grown-ups to be the candidates for an important job.

Who is Mr. Trump?

Now, let's talk about a special grown-up named Mr. Trump. He used to be the President of America! The President is like the biggest boss of the whole country. Even though he is not the President anymore, many people in America still like him very, very much, especially the people on 'Team Red', also known as the Republican Party. Mr. Trump is still very important to 'Team Red'. Think of it like a famous coach or a very popular older brother who everyone listens to. When he tells people who he thinks should be chosen for a job, many people on his team listen to his advice. He has a lot of 'pull', which means people pay attention to what he says and often follow his ideas. Did you know? The President of America lives in a very famous house called the White House!

Choosing Helpers for the 'Senate'

America has a very important group of grown-ups called the 'Senate'. These grown-ups work together to help make big rules for the entire country. They are like a council of wise elders who discuss important things and help the President make good decisions. It's a very big job! When people try to get one of these important jobs in the 'Senate', it's called a 'Senate Race'. It's not a running race, but a competition to see who gets the job. So, in Indiana and Ohio, grown-ups were choosing people to run for these important 'Senate' jobs. Did you know? In many countries, grown-ups vote to choose their leaders. This is called democracy!

Mr. Trump's Big Influence

So, what happened in Indiana and Ohio was quite interesting! Mr. Trump told people on 'Team Red' who he thought should get the jobs in the Senate. He said, "I think *this* person is very good! You should choose them!" And guess what? Most of the people he liked, the people he said were good, actually won their 'try-out' races! This shows that many people on 'Team Red' still trust Mr. Trump's ideas a lot. They listen to him and choose the people he suggests. It means he still has a lot of 'pull' or influence within his team. Even though he is not the President, he is still a very important voice for many people in America.

What Happens Next?

Now that the 'try-outs' are over in Indiana and Ohio, the teams have chosen their best players. These chosen people will now prepare for the *main* big game. This main game will happen later in the year and is called a 'midterm election'. It's an election that happens in the middle of when the current President is working. In this 'midterm election', the chosen people from 'Team Red' will compete against people from 'Team Blue' and other teams. Then, all the grown-ups in Indiana and Ohio will vote again, but this time it will be the *final* choice to pick who gets the actual important jobs in the 'Senate'. It's like the championship game after all the practice rounds! Did you know? The world has seven big continents, and America is in one called North America!

Why Does This Matter to Us?

Even though America is very far away, what happens there can sometimes be important for us too. The grown-ups who get these big jobs in America help make decisions about how America works with other countries, including countries here in Africa. They decide about sharing things, helping people, and making big plans for the world. So, it's always good to learn about what's happening in different parts of our amazing planet! It helps us understand how our big world works and how everyone is connected. Thank you for listening, my friends! See you next time with more exciting news from around the globe!
summary: Grown-ups in America held 'try-out' elections in Indiana and Ohio to choose who will run for important helper jobs, showing that many people still listen to former President Mr. Trump's advice.
whyMatters: Learning about America's choices is important because their leaders make big decisions that can affect other countries, including ours in Africa, and it helps us understand our big, connected world.

content: Soraya Lennie discusses false claims about Iran using marine life in warfare.

originalTitle: Shoot Palestinians, not settlers: Israeli general exposes double standard
title: Same Rules for Everyone is Best!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here! Today, we're going to talk about something super important. It's about being fair and treating everyone with kindness. We all want to live in a happy world, right? And fairness helps make that happen! Let's learn together about why fair rules are so, so important for everyone.

What Does "Fair" Mean?

Imagine you are playing your favourite game with your friends. Maybe it's hide-and-seek, or maybe it's building a tall tower with blocks. Every game has rules, doesn't it? Rules help us play nicely together. They make sure everyone has fun and knows what to do. Now, imagine if the rules were different for some players. What if one friend could have extra turns, but another friend couldn't? That wouldn't feel fair, would it? It might make some friends feel sad or left out. Fair means that everyone gets the same chance and follows the same rules. Did you know? Fairness is like sharing your favourite toy so that every friend gets a turn to play! It makes everyone feel good. When we are fair, everyone feels important. Everyone feels like they belong. And that's a wonderful feeling for all of us.

A Big Secret Came Out!

Sometimes, grown-ups have to make rules for many, many people. These rules help everyone live together safely in big communities. Just like your parents make rules for your home, grown-ups make rules for towns and cities. Recently, someone very important, a leader among grown-ups, said something special. They shared a secret that made some people think very carefully. This leader said that sometimes, the rules were not always the same for *everyone*. Imagine that! It was like some groups of people had one kind of rule. And other groups of people, maybe living very close by, had a different kind of rule. Even if they did the exact same thing, the rules might have treated them differently. It's a bit like if you and your friend both accidentally spilled some juice, but only one of you had to clean it up! That wouldn't feel quite right. This made many people feel sad and think hard. Because everyone wants to be treated with kindness and have the same rules applied to them. It's important for everyone to feel safe and equal, no matter who they are or where they live.

Why Are Fair Rules So Important?

When rules are fair, it's like a big warm blanket of safety and happiness for everyone. Everyone feels calm and cared for. They know what to expect. Fair rules help everyone get along better. When people feel treated fairly, they are more likely to smile, share, and play together. Did you know? When everyone is treated fairly, it helps people become good friends and work together to make amazing things happen! Unfair rules can make people feel very sad. They might feel angry, or confused, or even left out. Nobody wants to feel that way, do they? We want everyone to feel happy and included. We want everyone to know that they are important. Fair rules help us achieve this wonderful feeling of belonging. When everyone has the same fair rules, it helps to stop arguments. It helps everyone understand what is expected of them, and that makes life much easier and happier for all.

What Can We Do?

Even as little ones, there's so much we can do! We can start by being fair in our own games and with our own friends. We can share our toys and listen to everyone's ideas. We can talk to our grown-ups about what is fair. If something doesn't feel fair, it's good to talk about it with a trusted grown-up. They can help understand and make things better. We can always try our best to be kind to everyone we meet. A kind word or a helping hand can make a big difference in someone's day. Did you know? Using kind words and really listening to others are big parts of being fair! It helps us understand how others feel. It's super important to remember that everyone, no matter where they come from or what they look like, wants to be treated with respect and fairness. Just like you want to be treated fairly by your friends and family, other children and grown-ups around the world want the same.

Making Our World a Happy Place

When everyone has fair rules, our communities become much stronger and happier. People can play, learn, and grow together without unnecessary worries. It helps every child grow up feeling safe, loved, and important. And that's what we want for all children, everywhere! Did you know? Many grown-ups around the world work very hard every single day to try and make sure everyone has fair chances and fair rules! They believe in fairness too. We all want a world where kindness, love, and fairness shine bright like the sun. It's a goal that we can all work towards, little by little, every day. So, let's promise to always try our best to be fair to our friends, our family, and everyone we meet. Let's make our world a truly wonderful place, filled with fairness for all. Thank you for listening, my little friends! Keep shining brightly and remember to always be fair!
summary: Recently, it was revealed that sometimes rules are not fair for everyone, making some people sad, but it's important that everyone is treated kindly and with the same rules so we can all live happily together.
whyMatters: Fairness matters because when everyone follows the same rules and is treated kindly, people feel safe, happy, and can be good friends, making the world a much better place for all.

originalTitle: Ted Turner, media mogul who revolutionised TV news by launching CNN, dies at 87
title: A Special Man Who Changed TV News Forever
content:
Hello, amazing friends! Today, we have a story about a very special man named Mr. Ted Turner. He lived a very long life, and he just went to rest at 87 years old. We remember him because he did something truly amazing for TV news!

Who Was Mr. Ted Turner?

Mr. Ted Turner was like a wizard of television! He loved TV and had many big ideas. He helped create many TV channels and shows. People sometimes called him a "media mogul." This just means he owned many, many TV and news companies. He had a big imagination and loved trying new things!

What is TV News?

News is like a big story about what is happening in our world. It tells us about new things, important events, and people. News helps us learn about our community, our country, and other places far away. Before Mr. Ted, TV news was only shown for a little while each day. You had to wait to hear the news.

News Before Mr. Ted's Big Idea

Imagine this: you would turn on the TV for news, but it was only on for a short time, maybe for 30 minutes in the morning. Then, it would go away! If something big happened, like a special event or a new discovery, you had to wait until the next news time to hear about it. It was like waiting for your favourite cartoon to start at a special time. Did you know? Before Mr. Ted, most news channels would only show news for short times, like a few minutes in the morning and evening! You couldn't watch news all day long.

The Super Idea: CNN!

Then, in a special year called 1980, Mr. Ted had a super-duper idea! He wanted to make a TV channel that showed news all the time. Every single minute! He called it CNN. CNN stands for Cable News Network. He wanted people to know what was happening in the world, whenever they wanted to know!

News All Day and All Night!

CNN was the very first channel in the whole wide world to show news 24 hours a day, 7 days a week! This means you could turn on the TV anytime – morning, afternoon, evening, or even late at night – and guess what? News would be on! It was like magic! This was a totally new way to watch TV. Did you know? CNN was the very first TV channel in the world to show news every single minute, all day and all night! No other channel did that before, making Mr. Ted a true pioneer!

How Mr. Ted Changed TV News

This was a huge change! Before CNN, if something important happened, you might not know about it until many hours later. But with CNN, everyone could know what was happening right away! It was like having a super fast messenger always telling you the latest stories from everywhere in the world.

News from Everywhere

Because of Mr. Ted's big idea, people all over the world could watch news from different places. They could learn about what was happening in countries far away, like here in Africa, or in Europe, or America. This helped everyone understand each other better. It brought the world closer, just by showing us more! Did you know? Thanks to Mr. Ted and CNN, people in faraway countries could see what was happening in Africa, and people in Africa could see what was happening on the other side of the world, live as it happened!

Always On, Always Learning

This "24-hour news" idea meant we could always be learning. If there was a big event, like a special festival or an important meeting of leaders, CNN would show it live! Live means it's happening right now, as you watch it! It made the world feel much smaller and easier to understand.

Ted Turner's Big Heart

Mr. Ted Turner was not just about TV. He also loved our planet very much. He cared a lot about protecting nature and helping people around the world. He wanted everyone to live in a peaceful and happy world. He did many good things for our Earth. Did you know? Mr. Ted Turner also helped save animals and nature by giving lots of money to protect our beautiful Earth! He had a very kind heart and wanted to make the world a better place for everyone.

A Long and Wonderful Life

Mr. Ted lived for 87 years. That's a very long time! He saw many changes in the world, and he made many of those changes happen himself. He was a very smart and brave person who wasn't afraid to try new things and make his dreams come true.

His Gift to Us

His gift to all of us is that we can now get news whenever we want it. We can stay informed about our homes, our country, and even what is happening far, far away. This helps us understand our world better and feel connected to people everywhere.

Thank You, Mr. Ted!

So, even though Mr. Ted Turner has left us, his amazing idea, CNN, is still here. And because of him, many other news channels now show news all day too! He showed everyone that news could be always on, always fresh, and always there to help us learn. We say thank you, Mr. Ted, for changing how we watch and learn about our world!
summary: Ted Turner, a very smart man, started CNN, the first TV channel to show news all day and all night, changing how everyone learns about the world by making information always available.
whyMatters: This is important because now we can always know what is happening in our world, anytime we want, helping us learn, understand different places, and stay connected to people everywhere.

originalTitle: Massive Alaska megatsunami was second largest ever recorded
title: Massive Alaska megatsunami was second largest ever recorded
content: New research suggests glacier melt driven by climate change is increasing the risk of giant waves.

title: Injury row, yacht trip & petition - what's going on with Mbappe?
content: It's been a difficult second season for Kylian Mbappe at Real Madrid. What's gone wrong?

originalTitle: Was Arsenal ecstasy justified or a 'bit too much'?
title: Was Arsenal ecstasy justified or a 'bit too much'?
content: Arsenal have reached the Champions League final for the first time in 20 years but did they over-celebrate Tuesday's win over Atletico Madrid?

originalTitle: Celtic could win double - but is change inevitable?
title: Celtic could win double - but is change inevitable?
content: Celtic could yet have another successful domestic season despite a rancorous and turbulent campaign. But is lots of change at the club inevitable even with success?
